patches.summary: Adds brand-vs-nonbrand split and page query workflow commands.
patches.reason: These read-only shortcuts turn common SEO Search Analytics tasks into agent-friendly commands without hand-building filters or classifiers.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./..., go vet ./..., and go build ./... pass from the Google Search Console CLI directory.

patches.summary: Add real OAuth login flow (loopback + PKCE S256) replacing the pasted-token UX. Wires the token endpoint that the Press left empty and adds auth login/set-client/forget commands.
patches.reason: The Printing Press emitted half a refresh-token flow (config fields, refreshAccessToken function, SaveTokens/ClearTokens helpers) but left tokenURL hardcoded empty, so refresh was a silent no-op. Users had to re-paste a 1-hour token from the OAuth Playground every hour. Filed upstream as cli-printing-press#1200 so future OAuth CLIs ship with a working login flow by default.
patches.validated_outcome: go build, go vet, go test ./... all clean. Live: auth login flow completes end-to-end, tokens persist, auto-refresh works on natural and forced expiry, webmasters list-sites returns real data.

patches.summary: Fix silent data loss: every non-GET API request was calling os.RemoveAll on the shared cache directory, which deleted store.db right after sync wrote to it. Moved HTTP response cache into a `responses/` subdirectory so the analytics store is untouched.
patches.reason: The Printing Press generated a Client that puts its HTTP response cache at the same path as the Store package's SQLite DB. invalidateCache() runs after every POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH and does `os.RemoveAll(cacheDir)`. Sync POSTs to /searchAnalytics/query so every sync silently deleted store.db immediately after writing it. Generalizable Printing Press bug: any printed CLI that combines Client (with response cache) + Store (with SQLite) at the same canonical cache path has the same trap.
patches.validated_outcome: After fix: 21-day sync persists 23,250 rows, quick-wins/cliff/compare/historical all return real data. Response cache .json files now live in `~/.cache/<cli>/responses/` while store.db lives at `~/.cache/<cli>/store.db`. POST requests safely invalidate only response JSON.
